concerns.text: Task T033 implements 'permutation testing' but omits the explicit GLM beta-estimation step required by FR-003. FR-003 mandates 'perform General Linear Model (GLM) analysis to test for activation differences... specifically modeling parametric modulation'. T033 only handles the inference (permutation/FDR), leaving the core GLM estimation (beta extraction for feedback discrepancy) without a dedicated task, violating the requirement to perform the analysis.

Task T025b generates a 'convergence_report.json' but fails to include a specific verification step to calculate the global convergence rate and assert it meets the ≥90% threshold mandated by SC-002. The task description says 'aggregate convergence logs' but does not explicitly state the logic to compute the rate against the N_valid count and flag failure if <90%, effectively dropping the acceptance criterion.

Task T036a describes sweeping thresholds on 'alpha parameter and feedback discrepancy', which semantically weakens FR-006. FR-006 explicitly requires sweeping the 'belief-updating threshold/cutoff' to verify robustness of the headline correlation. Sweeping the model parameters themselves (alpha) or the input signal (discrepancy) is a different analysis than sweeping the *cutoff* used to filter or bin participants/conditions for the stability check.

Task T024 implements the model fitting logic but lacks a verification step to enforce the runtime constraints (≤6h on 2 CPU) and sample limits specified in the plan and SC-002 context. Without a task to dynamically adjust samples or enforce the timeout, the implementation risks violating the compute feasibility constraints defined in the plan, which are tied to the success criteria of the study.
